description: My balcony gardenia has disappeared again. We don’t have a knack for growing plants—we buy it, it blooms for a season, then it dies for a season, and the next batch... 😂
A flying dancer above Guanyin Mountain🪽—she’s really captivating when she takes off. Other than the dance part that looks great, everything else is pretty average. Worth going just once.
My keyboard setup guide⌨️, so I don’t keep forgetting how to configure it.
My wife buys a pile of 'local specialties' and stacks them at home—it reminds me of my hometown when I was little, where it was just like this 😂😂😂

After October every year, we go to Persimmon Village🍊 to play. This time we bought a string of them. They hang there the whole time—at first you can’t really eat them, but now they’re finally ready. Still looks beautiful while it hangs💕

An embroidered Cantonese-style white goose—bright the moment you see it🥰! The reflective side is really like the goose feathers I used to pick as a kid. One side feels really smooth and it also reflects🤩
A ceramic peach tree that looks really comforting.
Gulong Gorge is great fun—come here and you’ll know. The accommodation is also pretty cheap😃😘

Guangzhou’s attractions are densely packed—old-school charm, clean, and lots of foreigners. The malls are just like Shenzhen: repetitive, bright storefronts. Qingyuan’s malls, on the other hand, have far more variety. I found great food places, and they’re also cheaper.
Tea gardens. I didn’t see that kind of tea plantation view stretching across the hills.

Getting to know Xiamen from the sea is truly romantic. With elegant music and a joyful atmosphere, you see Xiamen’s most beautiful night view🥰. In this kind of setting, everything sounds great—the songs, the saxophone, and the violin really are wonderful😘.
Shantou’s Little Park. It’s a big featured neighborhood area, plus there’s a giant screen. It should be lively, but on New Year’s Eve there were surprisingly not many people.
